---
title: "{{< var icon.computer-activity >}} Activity"
subtitle: "Discussion 1, {{< var course.code >}}, {{< var course.term >}}"
---

## Learning goal

Work together to build a shared AI 100 code of conduct: the norms we will use to discuss sensitive, controversial, or personal AI topics with care, clarity, and accountability.

By the end of this activity, you should be able to identify behaviours that support respectful discussion, draft and refine code-of-conduct principles, and apply them to realistic scenarios.

## Time estimate

~40 minutes

## Group size

Small groups of 3–4 students, with several whole-class steps.

## {{< var icon.resource >}} Materials

* Sticky notes or index cards (physical), or a shared Miro board
* A shared document, whiteboard, or chart paper
* [AI 100 Code of Conduct](discussion-01_activity/code-of-conduct.md) (reference)
* [Lab 01 slides](../slides/discussion-01_slides.qmd)

## {{< var icon.task >}} Steps

1. **Individual reflection (5 min).** On sticky notes, jot behaviours or rules that helped or harmed past group discussions, and one principle you would want in this course. (Prefer to type or dictate? That works too.)
2. **Small-group drafting (10 min).** In your group, cluster your sticky notes into themes and turn each cluster into a clear, actionable principle.
3. **Merge into one class code (8 min).** Going around the room, each group adds a principle not already listed until the class has a single draft code of conduct.
4. **Scenario testing (8 min).** As a class, test the draft against a few realistic scenarios and revise where needed.
5. **Wrap-up (4 min).** The teaching team consolidates and publishes the final class code of conduct after the activity.

## Exit ticket

Complete a short reflection in PrairieLearn after the activity:

> What is one responsibility you have when discussing sensitive AI ethics topics in this course?
